name: Point-based accessibility and nearby POIs
when_to_use: Estimate what is reachable around a geocoded address or point, count nearby POIs/buildings, or make a small-radius fallback when place-wide OSM queries are too slow.
verified_snippet:
import osmnx as ox

ox.settings.use_cache = True
ox.settings.requests_timeout = 60
query = '{place_or_address}'
lat, lon = ox.geocode(query)
point = (lat, lon)
G = ox.graph_from_point(point, dist=1000, network_type='walk', simplify=True)
tags = {'amenity': True}
pois = ox.features_from_point(point, tags=tags, dist=1000)
print(f'lat={lat:.6f}, lon={lon:.6f}')
print(f'walk_nodes_1km={G.number_of_nodes()}')
print(f'walk_edges_1km={G.number_of_edges()}')
print(f'amenities_1km={len(pois)}')
io_contract: Input: address/place query and radius in meters. Output: geocoded coordinate, network node/edge counts, nearby feature count.
pitfalls: osmnx.geocode returns (lat, lon); nearest_nodes expects lon, lat.
Use graph_from_point for dist; graph_from_place has no dist parameter.
Counts are OSM-derived and should be described as approximate.
